svg.title: Enforcement — what the gate actually checks
svg.desc: Enforcement escalates from cheap static lints to deeper proofs. Static pre-merge lints check the guardrail.yaml is present, its schema is valid, the project code makes no direct provider-SDK calls, and every response is routed through the central utility. Beyond static: a behavioural test feeds a known-unsupported answer and asserts the gate trips; an architectural backstop removes the project's model API keys and egress so bypass is impossible not just detectable; and a quarterly red-team proves the grounding check catches fabrications.
svg.text: Enforcement — what the gate checks
Cheap structural lints first; then the checks that catch the clever and the probabilistic.
STATIC — pre-merge lints
deterministic · runs on every PR · milliseconds
File present
guardrail.yaml exists in the repo
Schema valid
required keys, sane values — grounding: on, threshold in range,
on_trip fallback non-empty
No direct LLM calls (scans the code)
bans imports/calls to raw provider SDKs — anthropic, boto3
bedrock invoke, openai. Every model call must go via central.
Every response routed
the user-facing path returns central's checked output,
never raw model text
These catch the honest mistake
Fast and deterministic — they block a merge that forgot the gate,
wired the wrong path, or reached for the raw SDK. But a determined
dev could still route around a static scan. So lints aren't the end.
BEYOND STATIC — lints can be gamed
proof, not just presence
Behavioural test
an integration test feeds a known-unsupported answer and
asserts the gate trips → fallback. Proves the wiring works, not
just that it exists.
Architectural backstop
project code has no model API keys and no network egress to
provider endpoints — only the central utility does. Bypass isn't
detected, it's impossible.
Quarterly efficacy red-team
fires fabrications and adversarial questions at the gate and
measures catch-rate — grounding is probabilistic, so no lint
can certify it works.
These handle the determined and the probabilistic
Lints prove the gate is declared and wired. The behavioural test
proves it fires; the backstop makes skipping it impossible; the
red-team proves it actually catches what matters.
